title: Lepidocoryphantha macromeris
intro: Lepidocoryphantha macromeris, also known as the giant cluster barrel cactus, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in the treatment of colds, fever, and respiratory issues due to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Lepidocoryphantha macromeris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, this plant is often prepared in the form of tinctures, teas, and infusions, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions.

Lepidocoryphantha macromeris is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Lepidocoryphantha macromeris is further supported by its specific characteristics, such as its globular stems and central spines.
